description: |-
  Manages a Discord Auto Moderation rule. AutoMod scans messages and member profile updates for violations and runs configured actions (block, alert, timeout). Per-guild limits enforced server-side: max 6 KEYWORD rules and one each of SPAM/KEYWORD_PRESET/MENTION_SPAM/MEMBER_PROFILE.

# discord_auto_moderation_rule (Resource)

Manages a Discord Auto Moderation rule. AutoMod scans messages and member profile updates for violations and runs configured actions (block, alert, timeout). Per-guild limits enforced server-side: max 6 KEYWORD rules and one each of SPAM/KEYWORD_PRESET/MENTION_SPAM/MEMBER_PROFILE.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Built-in profanity / sexual content / slurs filter, alerting a mod log channel.
resource "discord_auto_moderation_rule" "offensive_language" {
  server_id    = var.server_id
  name         = "Offensive language"
  event_type   = 1 # MESSAGE_SEND
  trigger_type = 4 # KEYWORD_PRESET

  trigger_metadata {
    presets = [1, 2, 3] # profanity, sexual_content, slurs
  }

  exempt_roles = var.moderator_role_ids

  actions {
    type = 1 # BLOCK_MESSAGE
    metadata {
      custom_message = "Message blocked. Offensive language is not allowed."
    }
  }
  actions {
    type = 2 # SEND_ALERT_MESSAGE
    metadata {
      channel_id = var.mod_log_channel_id
    }
  }
}

# Mention-spam protection with a 5-mention cap.
resource "discord_auto_moderation_rule" "mention_spam" {
  server_id    = var.server_id
  name         = "Anti mention spam"
  event_type   = 1
  trigger_type = 5 # MENTION_SPAM

  trigger_metadata {
    mention_total_limit             = 5
    mention_raid_protection_enabled = true
  }

  exempt_roles = var.moderator_role_ids

  actions {
    type = 1
    metadata {
      custom_message = "Too many mentions in a single message."
    }
  }
  actions {
    type = 2
    metadata {
      channel_id = var.mod_log_channel_id
    }
  }
}

# Regex link filter — blocks any http(s) link.
resource "discord_auto_moderation_rule" "link_filter" {
  server_id    = var.server_id
  name         = "Link filter"
  event_type   = 1
  trigger_type = 1 # KEYWORD

  trigger_metadata {
    regex_patterns = ["https?://"]
  }

  exempt_roles = var.moderator_role_ids

  actions {
    type = 1
    metadata {
      custom_message = "Links are not allowed. Contact a moderator."
    }
  }
  actions {
    type = 2
    metadata {
      channel_id = var.mod_log_channel_id
    }
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `actions` (Block List, Min: 1) Actions to run when the rule is triggered. At least one action is required. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--actions))
- `event_type` (Number) When the rule fires: `1` = MESSAGE_SEND (message is sent or edited), `2` = MEMBER_UPDATE (member's profile changes).
- `name` (String) Display name of the rule.
- `server_id` (String) ID of the guild this rule applies to.
- `trigger_type` (Number) What kind of content triggers the rule. Discord does not allow editing this on an existing rule, so any change forces a replace. `1` = KEYWORD, `3` = SPAM, `4` = KEYWORD_PRESET, `5` = MENTION_SPAM, `6` = MEMBER_PROFILE.

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the rule is active.
- `exempt_channels` (Set of String) Channel IDs where this rule is not enforced.
- `exempt_roles` (Set of String) Role IDs whose members bypass this rule.
- `trigger_metadata` (Block List, Max: 1) Configuration specific to the chosen `trigger_type`. Required for trigger types 1, 4, 5, and 6. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--trigger_metadata))

### Read-Only

- `creator_id` (String) ID of the user who created the rule (set by Discord at creation).
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

<a id="nestedblock--actions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `actions`

Required:

- `type` (Number) `1` = BLOCK_MESSAGE, `2` = SEND_ALERT_MESSAGE, `3` = TIMEOUT (requires `MODERATE_MEMBERS`), `4` = BLOCK_MEMBER_INTERACTION (MEMBER_PROFILE rules only).

Optional:

- `metadata` (Block List, Max: 1) Action-specific metadata. Required for types 2 (channel_id) and 3 (duration_seconds); optional for type 1 (custom_message); ignored for type 4. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--actions--metadata))

<a id="nestedblock--actions--metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `actions.metadata`

Optional:

- `channel_id` (String) Channel to send alerts to. Required when action `type = 2` (SEND_ALERT_MESSAGE).
- `custom_message` (String) Custom block message shown to the user. Only meaningful when action `type = 1` (BLOCK_MESSAGE).
- `duration_seconds` (Number) Timeout duration in seconds (max 2419200 = 28 days). Required when action `type = 3` (TIMEOUT).



<a id="nestedblock--trigger_metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `trigger_metadata`

Optional:

- `allow_list` (Set of String) Substrings exempted from triggering the rule. Used by KEYWORD (1), KEYWORD_PRESET (4), and MEMBER_PROFILE (6).
- `keyword_filter` (Set of String) Substrings that trigger the rule. Used by KEYWORD (1) and MEMBER_PROFILE (6).
- `mention_raid_protection_enabled` (Boolean) Whether to detect mention-raid behaviour. Only valid for MENTION_SPAM (5).
- `mention_total_limit` (Number) Maximum number of unique role and user mentions allowed per message. Only valid for MENTION_SPAM (5).
- `presets` (Set of Number) Discord-built preset categories: `1` = profanity, `2` = sexual content, `3` = slurs. Only valid for KEYWORD_PRESET (4).
- `regex_patterns` (Set of String) Rust-flavor regex patterns that trigger the rule. Used by KEYWORD (1) and MEMBER_PROFILE (6).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import discord_auto_moderation_rule.example "<server id>:<rule id>"
```
